payload.title: Public GitHub repo + CI
payload.description:
Push the repo to public GitHub (under bri's account or an org she picks) and stand up CI so visitors see a green badge.

Tasks:
- Decide owner + repo name (default: github.com/briannadoubt/trust unless bri picks an org).
- Initial push: git remote add, push main, set as default branch.
- GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  - cargo fmt --check
  - cargo clippy -- -D warnings (probably; check what the repo currently tolerates)
  - cargo test --workspace
  - cargo trust check on case-studies/* (requires the wrapper built — bake the build step in)
- README badges: build status, license, crates.io (link will be live after RT-58).
- Branch protection on main: require CI green to merge. Skippable in solo mode but worth setting up.
- Decide what to gitignore that currently isn't: .venv/, anything else surfaced by 'git ls-files | head'.

Action requires bri's GitHub auth — not something an agent can do unattended. Ticket can be planned out fully and then bri runs the push step.
